source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two linearly polarized monochromatic waves with wavelengths $\lambda_{1}=\lambda_{2}=0.63 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ propagate at an angle $\theta=45 \mathrm{deg}$ to each other. Photosensitive plate P is placed perpendicular to the propagation direction of one of the waves. Determine the resolution $R$ [lines $/ \mathrm{mm}$ ] of the photosensitive plate required for recording the pattern of arising interference fringes.
extraction_notes: image field contains PIL object string representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs
force_requires_image set true based on record metadata including vision_relevance=essential and accompanying diagram caption

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate intended visual grounding
question_text already states the key geometry numerically (equal wavelengths, 45 deg crossing angle, plate perpendicular to one wave)
no answer choices or unlabeled visual references are present in the text, so the image may be helpful but does not appear strictly necessary
multi_step: solving requires combining wave-interference geometry with the recording-plate orientation
likely needs at least two steps: derive fringe spacing from wavelength/angle, then convert spacing to lines per mm resolution
answer_type is numeric with no choices, so the solver must compute rather than recognize
verifiability: answer_type=number with a single explicit target value ('2245')
physics formula path is standard and can be checked by recomputation from the given quantities
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true

risk_flags: metadata says requires_image=true, but the text appears mostly self-sufficient for solving
possible ambiguity in fringe-spacing formula depending on geometric interpretation of the plate/wave orientation
